response.justification: A strong, versatile engineer with deep experience across web, embedded, and systems work, plus rare directly-relevant IDE experience (C/C++ components for NI LabWindows/CVI). However, the role demands deep JVM expertise with Java, Kotlin, and the Spring ecosystem, and this candidate's core strengths are in TypeScript/Node.js and DLang. Java appears only in passing (robotic arms, device orchestration) with no Kotlin, no Spring, and no large-scale JVM tooling work. The general engineering caliber is high, but the specialized stack alignment is weak.
strengths: Direct IDE/developer-tooling experience (NI LabWindows/CVI in C/C++; CI/CD and dev-tooling infra)
Proven on complex, high-performance systems (low-latency micro-services handling millions of req/s)
Strong software craftsmanship: TDD, pairing, trunk-based development, plus authored test libraries
concerns: No demonstrated Spring/Quarkus/Hibernate or enterprise-framework experience
Minimal Java and zero Kotlin depth; core expertise is JS/TS and DLang, not the JVM
Career focus is full-stack/web rather than language tooling and IDE internals
